---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
lbispats = read.xlsx("C://Users//joaop//Desktop//fava//lbispats.xlsx")
```

```{r}

# Função para rodar SpATS em um ambiente/trait específico
run_spats <- function(var_i, env_i) {
  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("Rodando:", var_i, "no ambiente", env_i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")
  
  # Filtra o ambiente
  lbindet_t <- lbindet %>% filter(env == env_i)
  
  ncol_i <- max(lbindet_t$Column, na.rm = TRUE)
  nrow_i <- max(lbindet_t$Row, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Converte fatores
  lbindet_t$Block <- as.factor(lbindet_t$Block)
  lbindet_t$R     <- as.factor(lbindet_t$Row)
  lbindet_t$C     <- as.factor(lbindet_t$Column)
  lbindet_t$G     <- as.factor(lbindet_t$Genotype)
  lbindet_t$e     <- as.factor(lbindet_t$env)
  
  nseg1i <- ncol_i
  nseg2i <- nrow_i
  
  # Ajuste do modelo
  mod_spats <- SpATS(response = var_i,
                     spatial = ~ PSANOVA(Column, Row,
                                         nseg = c(nseg1i, nseg2i),
                                         pord = 2,
                                         degree = 3,
                                         nest.div = c(2,1)),
                     genotype = "G", genotype.as.random = TRUE,
                     fixed = ~ Block,
                     random = ~ R + C,
                     data = lbindet_t,
                     control = list(tolerance = 1e-06))
  
  # Resumos
  print(summary(mod_spats, which = "all"))
  
  # Plot
  plot(mod_spats, main = paste(env_i, "-", var_i))
  
  return(mod_spats)
}

# ====================================================
# Rodar todos os traits/ambientes que você enviou
# ====================================================

# E01
mod_GLI_E01  <- run_spats("GLI", "E01")
mod_BGI_E01  <- run_spats("BGI", "E01")

# E02
mod_BGI_E02  <- run_spats("BGI", "E02")

# E03
mod_BGI_E03  <- run_spats("BGI", "E03")

# E04
mod_BGI_E04  <- run_spats("BGI", "E04")
mod_GLI_E04  <- run_spats("GLI", "E04")

# E05
mod_AreaPercentage_E05 <- run_spats("%Canopy", "E05")
mod_GLI_E05            <- run_spats("GLI", "E05")
mod_BGI_E05            <- run_spats("BGI", "E05")
mod_NGRDI_E05          <- run_spats("NGRDI", "E05")
mod_VARI_E05           <- run_spats("VARI", "E05")

# E06
mod_BGI_E06   <- run_spats("BGI", "E06")
mod_GLI_E06   <- run_spats("GLI", "E06")
mod_NGRDI_E06 <- run_spats("NGRDI", "E06")
mod_VARI_E06  <- run_spats("VARI", "E06")

# Independente do ambiente (OneHundredSeedWeight) → roda em todos
for (e in unique(lbindet$env)) {
  assign(paste0("mod_HSW_", e),
         run_spats("HSW", e))
}

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Dados em formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(R, C, `f(Column)`, `f(Row)`, 
                        `f(Column):Row`, `Column:f(Row)`, `f(Column):f(Row)`),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    DAS = factor(DAS, levels = sort(unique(DAS))), # x como categórico
    Env = as.character(Env)
  )

# Gráfico
ggplot(lbispats_long, aes(x = DAS, y = Value,
                          color = Component,
                          group = interaction(Component, Env))) +
  geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +  # linhas conectando por componente/env
  geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 3, stroke = 1.2) +  # pontos só nos Env
  facet_wrap(~Trait, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15,  # quadrado cheio
                                "25m"   = 16,  # círculo cheio
                                "*"     = 8)) + # asterisco
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "right") +
  labs(x = "DAS", y = "EDk",
       title = "Effective Dimensions")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Reorganizar dados em formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(R, C, `f(Column)`, `f(Row)`, 
                        `f(Column):Row`, `Column:f(Row)`, `f(Column):f(Row)`),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    DAS = factor(DAS, levels = unique(DAS)), # DAS como fator (tempo categórico)
    Env = as.character(Env)
  )

# lista de traits únicos
traits <- unique(lbispats_long$Trait)

# diretório para salvar
dir.create("plots_traits_EDk", showWarnings = FALSE)

# loop para gerar os 9 gráficos
for(tr in traits){
  
  df_trait <- lbispats_long %>% filter(Trait == tr)
  
  p <- ggplot(df_trait, aes(x = DAS, y = Value,
                            color = Component,
                            group = interaction(Component, Env))) +
    geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 3, stroke = 1.2) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15,  # quadrado cheio
                                  "25m"   = 16,  # círculo cheio
                                  "*"     = 8)) + # asterisco
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "right") +
    labs(x = "DAS",
         y = "EDk",
         title = paste(tr, "- Effective Dimension"))
  
  print(p)}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# Reorganizar dados em formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(R, C, `f(Column)`, `f(Row)`, 
                        `f(Column):Row`, `Column:f(Row)`, `f(Column):f(Row)`),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    DAS = as.character(DAS), # tratar como string para poder reordenar
    Env = as.character(Env)
  )

# lista de traits únicos
traits <- unique(lbispats_long$Trait)

# ordem do eixo X
ordem_DAS <- c("23","40","64","118-133")

# ordem dos componentes
ordem_comp <- c("R","C","f(Column)","f(Row)","f(Column):Row","Column:f(Row)","f(Column):f(Row)")

# paleta de cores mais contrastante
cores <- brewer.pal(7,"Set1")


# loop para gerar os 9 gráficos
for(tr in traits){
  
  df_trait <- lbispats_long %>% filter(Trait == tr)
  
  # ajustar ordem dos DAS de acordo com os níveis que existem no Trait
  df_trait <- df_trait %>% 
    mutate(DAS = factor(DAS, levels = intersect(ordem_DAS, unique(DAS))))
  
  p <- ggplot(df_trait, aes(x = DAS, y = Value,
                            color = Component,
                            group = interaction(Component, Env))) +
    geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 4, stroke = 3) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15,  # quadrado cheio
                                  "25m"   = 16,  # círculo cheio
                                  "*"     = 8)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = cores, breaks = ordem_comp) +
    theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  # remove linhas principais
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  # remove linhas secundárias
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face="bold"),
    aspect.ratio = 1.5
  ) +
    labs(x = "DAS",
         y = "EDk") +
    facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y")
  
  print(p)

}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)  # 👈 para juntar os gráficos

# Reorganizar dados em formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(R, C, `f(Column)`, `f(Row)`, 
                        `f(Column):Row`, `Column:f(Row)`, `f(Column):f(Row)`),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    DAS = as.character(DAS),
    Env = as.character(Env)
  )

# ordem do eixo X
ordem_DAS <- c("23","40","64","118-133")

# ordem dos componentes
ordem_comp <- c("R","C","f(Column)","f(Row)","f(Column):Row","Column:f(Row)","f(Column):f(Row)")

# paleta de cores contrastantes
cores <- brewer.pal(7,"Set1")

# Função para gerar um gráfico de um Trait específico
make_plot <- function(tr){
  df_trait <- lbispats_long %>% filter(Trait == tr)
  
  df_trait <- df_trait %>% 
    mutate(DAS = factor(DAS, levels = intersect(ordem_DAS, unique(DAS))))
  
  p <- ggplot(df_trait, aes(x = DAS, y = Value,
                            color = Component,
                            group = interaction(Component, Env))) +
    geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 3, stroke = 1.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15, "25m" = 16, "*" = 8)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = cores, breaks = ordem_comp) +
    theme_linedraw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "none",   # 👈 remove legenda individual
      plot.title = element_text(face="bold"),
      aspect.ratio = 1
    ) +
    labs(x = "DAS", y = "EDk") + facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y")
  return(p)
}

# Ordem desejada dos traits (3x3)
ordem_traits <- list(
  c("AUC_%Canopy", "AUC_BGI", "AUC_GLI"),
  c("%Canopy", "BGI", "GLI"),
  c("HSW (g)", "NGRDI", "VARI")
)

# Criar os 9 gráficos
plots <- lapply(unlist(ordem_traits), make_plot)

# Montar em 3 linhas x 3 colunas
layout <- "
ABC
DEF
GHI
"
final_plot <- wrap_plots(
  A = plots[[1]], B = plots[[2]], C = plots[[3]],
  D = plots[[4]], E = plots[[5]], F = plots[[6]],
  G = plots[[7]], H = plots[[8]], I = plots[[9]],
  design = layout
)

# Mostrar
print(final_plot)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)   # 👈 para manipular legendas

# Reorganizar dados em formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(R, C, `f(Column)`, `f(Row)`, 
                        `f(Column):Row`, `Column:f(Row)`, `f(Column):f(Row)`),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    DAS = as.character(DAS),
    Env = as.character(Env)
  )

# ordem do eixo X
ordem_DAS <- c("23","40","64","118-133")

# ordem dos componentes
ordem_comp <- c("R","C","f(Column)","f(Row)","f(Column):Row","Column:f(Row)","f(Column):f(Row)")

# paleta de cores contrastantes
cores = c("R" = "#6BBFFF", "C" = "#FFAC6B","f(Column)" = "#3DF200","f(Row)" = "#AB6BFF", "f(Column):Row" = "#FF6B75", "Column:f(Row)" = "#F2B600", "f(Column):f(Row)" = "#A35A00")

# Função para gerar um gráfico de um Trait específico
make_plot <- function(tr){
  df_trait <- lbispats_long %>% filter(Trait == tr)
  
  df_trait <- df_trait %>% 
    mutate(DAS = factor(DAS, levels = intersect(ordem_DAS, unique(DAS))))
  
  p <- ggplot(df_trait, aes(x = DAS, y = Value,
                            color = Component,
                            group = interaction(Component, Env))) +
    geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 3, stroke = 1.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15, "25m" = 16, "*" = 8), guide = "none") +  # 👈 esconde legenda de Env
    scale_color_manual(values = cores, breaks = ordem_comp) +
    theme_linedraw(base_size = 30) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),                  
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",   # 👈 sem legenda individual
      plot.title = element_text(face="bold"),
      aspect.ratio = 1
    ) +
    labs(x = "DAS", y = "EDk") +
    facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y")   # 👈 mantém fundo preto nos títulos
  return(p)
}

# Ordem desejada dos traits (3x3)
ordem_traits <- list(
  c("AUC_%Canopy", "AUC_BGI", "AUC_GLI"),
  c("%Canopy", "BGI", "GLI"),
  c("HSW (g)", "NGRDI", "VARI")
)

# Criar os 9 gráficos
plots <- lapply(unlist(ordem_traits), make_plot)

# Montar em 3 linhas x 3 colunas
layout <- "
ABC
DEF
GHI
"
main_plots <- wrap_plots(
  A = plots[[1]], B = plots[[2]], C = plots[[3]],
  D = plots[[4]], E = plots[[5]], F = plots[[6]],
  G = plots[[7]], H = plots[[8]], I = plots[[9]],
  design = layout
) 

# ---- Legenda de Component ----
legend_component <- get_legend(
  ggplot(lbispats_long, aes(x = DAS, y = Value, color = Component)) +
    geom_point() +
    scale_color_manual(values = cores, breaks = ordem_comp) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 36) +
    theme(
      legend.position   = "right",
      legend.title      = element_text(face="bold"),
      legend.margin     = margin(t=0, r=0, b=0, l=0, unit="pt"),
      legend.box.margin = margin(t=0, r=0, b=0, l=0, unit="pt"),
      legend.spacing.x  = unit(0, "pt"),
      legend.spacing.y  = unit(0, "pt"),
      plot.margin       = margin(t=0, r=0, b=0, l=0, unit="pt")
    )
)

# ---- Legenda de Env ----
legend_env <- get_legend(
  ggplot(lbispats_long, aes(x = DAS, y = Value, shape = Env)) +
    geom_point() +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15, "25m" = 16, "*" = 8)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 36) +
    theme(
      legend.position   = "right",
      legend.title      = element_text(face="bold"),
      legend.margin     = margin(t=0, r=0, b=0, l=0, unit="pt"),
      legend.box.margin = margin(t=0, r=0, b=0, l=0, unit="pt"),
      legend.spacing.x  = unit(0, "pt"),
      legend.spacing.y  = unit(0, "pt"),
      plot.margin       = margin(t=0, r=0, b=0, l=0, unit="pt")
    )
)

# ---- Empilhar as legendas verticalmente ----
combined_legend <- 
  legend_component/
  legend_env

# ---- Juntar gráfico principal + legenda combinada ----
final_plot <- main_plots | combined_legend

print(main_plots)
print(combined_legend)
final_plot
```

```{r}
ggsave(plot = main_plots, "main_plots.tiff", width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw",            # compressão sem perdas
  units = "in", bg = "transparent")
ggsave(plot = combined_legend, "combined_legend.tiff", width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw",            # compressão sem perdas
  units = "in", bg = "transparent")
```

```{r}
lbispats2 = read.xlsx("C://Users//joaop//Desktop//fava//models_indet2.xlsx")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Transformar os dados para formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats2 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(EDk, EDg, EDe),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value")

# Definir a ordem desejada
ordem_trait_env <- c(
  "GLI_E01", "BGI_E01", "BGI_E02", "BGI_E03", "BGI_E04", "GLI_E04",
  "%Canopy_E05", "GLI_E05", "BGI_E05", "NGRDI_E05", "VARI_E05",
  "BGI_E06", "GLI_E06", "NGRDI_E06", "VARI_E06",
  "AUC_BGI_12.5m", "AUC_%Canopy_12.5m", "AUC_BGI_25m", "AUC_GLI_25m",
  "HSW"
)

lbispats_long <- lbispats_long %>%
  mutate(Trait_Env = factor(Trait_Env, levels = ordem_trait_env))

cores1 <- c("EDk" = "#3559E0", "EDg" = "#F2B600","EDe" = "#80C14D" )

# Gráfico de linhas
eds = ggplot(lbispats_long, aes(x = Trait_Env, y = Value, color = Component, group = Component)) +
  geom_line(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 8) +
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = cores1) + 
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold", color = "black"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),                       
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size =24),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size =24),
    legend.text = element_text(face = "bold",color = "black", size = 24),
      legend.position = "right",  # 👈 sem legenda individual
      plot.title = element_text(face="bold")
    ) + 
  labs(x = "Trait / Environment", y = "Effective Dimension", color = "Component")
print(eds)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Renomear colunas problemáticas
lbispats2 <- lbispats2 %>%
  rename(
    h2 = `h²`,
    CVgCVe = `CVg/CVe`
  )

# Transformar os dados para formato longo
lbispats_long2 <- lbispats2 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(h2, CVgCVe),
               names_to = "Parameter",
               values_to = "Value")

# Definir a ordem desejada
ordem_trait_env <- c(
  "GLI_E01", "BGI_E01", "BGI_E02", "BGI_E03", "BGI_E04", "GLI_E04",
  "%Canopy_E05", "GLI_E05", "BGI_E05", "NGRDI_E05", "VARI_E05",
  "BGI_E06", "GLI_E06", "NGRDI_E06", "VARI_E06",
  "AUC_BGI_12.5m", "AUC_%Canopy_12.5m", "AUC_BGI_25m", "AUC_GLI_25m",
  "HSW"
)

lbispats_long2 <- lbispats_long2 %>%
  mutate(Trait_Env = factor(Trait_Env, levels = ordem_trait_env))

cores2 <- c("h2" = "#2E5BFF", "CVgCVe" = "#D1DFFF")
```

```{r}
h2_cvgcve = ggplot() +
  # Barras apenas para CVg/CVe
  geom_col(data = filter(lbispats_long2, Parameter == "CVgCVe"),
           aes(x = Trait_Env, y = Value, fill = Parameter),
           color = "black", position = "dodge", show.legend = F) +
  # Pontos em formato estrela para h²
  geom_point(data = filter(lbispats_long2, Parameter == "h2"),
             aes(x = Trait_Env, y = Value, color = Parameter),
             shape = 8, size = 3, stroke = 2) +   # shape = 8 → estrela
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = cores2,
    labels = c(CVgCVe = "CVg/CVe")   # legenda só da barra
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = cores2,
    labels = c(h2 = expression(bold(H^2)))  # legenda só do ponto
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1),  # ordem da legenda
    color = guide_legend(order = 2)
  ) +
  # Linha horizontal vermelha tracejada
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  # Escala do eixo Y de 0 a 1, de 0.2 em 0.2 (ajuste se precisar)
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 2.25, 0.5), limits = c(0, 2.25)) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),   # 👈 eixo Y em negrito
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title   = element_text(face="bold", size = 24)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Trait / Environment", 
       y = expression(bold(~CV[g]/CV[e])), 
       fill = "Parameter", 
       color = element_blank())

print(h2_cvgcve)
```

```{r}
eds = eds + ggtitle("A")
h2_cvgcve = h2_cvgcve + ggtitle("B")
joinplots1 = eds / h2_cvgcve
print(joinplots1)
```

```{r}
ggsave("joinplots1.tiff",joinplots1,  width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw",            # compressão sem perdas
  units = "in", bg = "transparent")
```

```{r}
blups_traits_sig2 = read.xlsx("C://Users//joaop//Desktop//fava//blups_traits_sig2.xlsx")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(FactoMineR)   # PCA
library(factoextra)   # Visualização da PCA

# Selecionar só os BLUPs numéricos
blups_num <- blups_traits_sig2 %>%
  select(-n) %>%                # remover coluna n
  column_to_rownames("Genotype")  # genótipo como rownames
```

```{r}
pca_res <- PCA(blups_num, scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Gráfico de indivíduos (genótipos)
fviz_pca_ind(pca_res,
             repel = TRUE,          # evita sobreposição
             col.ind = "blue",      # cor dos genótipos
             pointsize = 3,
             title = "PCA dos BLUPs dos Genótipos")

# Gráfico das variáveis
fviz_pca_var(pca_res,
             col.var = "red",
             repel = TRUE,
             title = "Contribuição das Variáveis para a PCA")

```

```{r}
pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$ind$coord)
pca_scores$Genotype <- rownames(pca_scores)

# Por exemplo, pegar os 5 melhores genótipos no PC1
top_genos <- pca_scores %>%
  arrange(desc(Dim.1)) %>%
  head(5)
top_genos

```

```{r}
cor_blups <- cor(blups_num)
library(corrplot)
corrplot(cor_blups, method = "color", tl.col = "black", tl.cex = 0.8, type = "lower")

```

```{r}
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)

# Preparar os dados: genótipos como rownames
blups_num <- blups_traits_sig2 %>%
  select(-n) %>%
  column_to_rownames("Genotype")

# Rodar PCA
pca_res <- PCA(blups_num, scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)

# Biplot integrado
fviz_pca_biplot(pca_res,
                repel = TRUE,           # evita sobreposição
                col.ind = "blue",       # cor dos genótipos
                col.var = "red",        # cor das variáveis
                pointsize = 3,          # tamanho dos pontos
                arrowsize = 1,          # tamanho das setas
                labelsize = 5,          # tamanho dos rótulos
                title = "Biplot PCA dos BLUPs")

```

```{r}
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# Preparar os dados: remover HSW e usar Genotype como rownames
blups_num <- blups_traits_sig2 %>%
  select(-n, -HSW) %>%           # remove n e HSW
  column_to_rownames("Genotype")  # define genótipos como rownames

# Rodar PCA
pca_res <- PCA(blups_num, scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)

# Biplot PCA
biplotlbi = fviz_pca_biplot(pca_res,
                repel = TRUE, 
                geom = c("text"),
                geom.var = c("text", "arrow"),
                col.ind = "#FF1E00",       # cor dos genótipos
                col.var = "#2E5BFF",        # cor das variáveis
                pointsize = 0,
                arrowsize = 1,
                labelsize = 6,
                title = NULL) + theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +  
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),   # 👈 eixo Y em negrito
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18)))
  biplotlbi
```

```{r}
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# Preparar os dados: remover HSW e usar Genotype como rownames
blups_num <- blups_traits_sig2 %>%
  select(-n, -HSW) %>%
  column_to_rownames("Genotype")

# Rodar PCA
pca_res <- PCA(blups_num, scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)

# Ver contribuição das variáveis no PC1 + PC2
var_contrib <- pca_res$var$contrib[,1] + pca_res$var$contrib[,2]

# Selecionar top 8 variáveis mais importantes
top_vars <- names(sort(var_contrib, decreasing = TRUE))[1:8]

# Biplot apenas com as 8 variáveis selecionadas
fviz_pca_biplot(pca_res,
                repel = TRUE,
                geom = c("text"),
                geom.var = c("text", "arrow"),
                select.var = list(name = top_vars),  # 👈 filtra variáveis
                col.ind = "#FF1E00",       # cor dos genótipos
                col.var = "#2E5BFF",       # cor das variáveis
                pointsize = 0,
                arrowsize = 3,
                labelsize = 2,
                title = "Biplot PCA (Top 8 variáveis)") +
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 16) +  
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

```

```{r}
pca_contrib = fviz_pca_var(
  pca_res,
  col.var = "contrib", # Colorir pelas contribuições
  gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB","purple","darkred"),
  repel = TRUE,
  col.circle = "grey77",
  select.var = list(contrib = 8), 
  title = "PCA traits contribution"
) +
  labs(title = NULL)
pca_contrib
```

```{r}
# Gráfico de contribuição para PC1
fviz_contrib(pca_res, choice = "var", axes = 1,fill = "lightblue", color = "black", top = 10) +
  labs(title = "Traits contribution for PC1")
# Gráfico de contribuição para PC2
fviz_contrib(pca_res, choice = "var", axes = 2,fill = "lightyellow", color = "black", top = 10) +
  labs(title = "Traits contribution for PC2")
fviz_contrib(pca_res, choice = "var", axes = 1:2,fill = "lightgrey", color = "black", top = 10) +
  labs(title = "Traits contribution for PC1 and PC2")
```

```{r}
# Pegar contribuições
var_contrib <- pca_res$var$contrib

# Ordenar pela contribuição total (Dim.1 + Dim.2) e pegar top 8
top_vars <- sort(rowSums(var_contrib[,1:2]), decreasing = TRUE)[1:8]
top_vars

```

```{r}
# Calcular contribuição total
var_contrib <- pca_res$var$contrib
top_vars <- sort(rowSums(var_contrib[,1:2]), decreasing = TRUE)[1:8]
top_names <- names(top_vars)

# Usar select.var corretamente
fviz_pca_var(
  pca_res,
  col.var = "contrib",
  gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB","purple","#FF1E00"),
  arrowsize = 1,
  labelsize = 5,
  repel = TRUE,
  col.circle = "grey77",
  select.var = list(name = top_names),  # 👈 precisa estar dentro de list(name = ...)
  title = "Top 8 PCA traits"
)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# 1. Loadings organizados em data.frame
loadings_df <- as.data.frame(pca_res$var$coord)
loadings_df$variavel <- rownames(loadings_df)

# 2. Função para pegar as top variáveis por PC
top_vars_por_pc <- function(pc, n = 5) {
  loadings_df %>%
    select(variavel, all_of(pc)) %>%
    mutate(abs_val = abs(!!sym(pc))) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs_val)) %>%
    dplyr::slice(1:n) %>%
    select(variavel, loading = all_of(pc)) %>%
    mutate(PC = pc)
}

# 3. Extrair para PC1, PC2, PC3 (ou quantos quiser)
top_pc1 <- top_vars_por_pc("Dim.1", n = 5)  # FactoMineR chama PC1 = "Dim.1"
top_pc2 <- top_vars_por_pc("Dim.2", n = 5)
top_pc3 <- top_vars_por_pc("Dim.3", n = 5)

# 4. Juntar todas
top_all <- bind_rows(top_pc1, top_pc2, top_pc3)

# 5. Variância explicada de cada PC (%)
var_exp <- pca_res$eig[,2]   # % explicada por cada dimensão
importancia_df <- tibble(
  PC = paste0("Dim.", 1:length(var_exp)),
  importancia = round(var_exp, 2) # em porcentagem
)

# 6. Juntar importância ao dataframe de top variáveis
top_all <- left_join(top_all, importancia_df, by = "PC")

# 7. Ordenar por ranking dentro de cada PC
top_all <- top_all %>%
  group_by(PC) %>%
  mutate(rank = rank(-abs(loading))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(PC, rank)

top_all

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(factoextra)

# 1. Função ajustada
top_vars_por_pc <- function(pc, n) {
  loadings_df %>%
    select(variavel, all_of(pc)) %>%
    mutate(abs_val = abs(!!sym(pc))) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs_val)) %>%
    dplyr::slice(1:n) %>%
    select(variavel, loading = all_of(pc)) %>%
    mutate(PC = pc)
}

# 2. Pegar só PC1 (5 variáveis) e PC2 (3 variáveis)
top_pc1 <- top_vars_por_pc("Dim.1", n = 5)
top_pc2 <- top_vars_por_pc("Dim.2", n = 3)

# 3. Juntar as duas
top_vars <- bind_rows(top_pc1, top_pc2)

# 4. Vetor de nomes das variáveis selecionadas
top_names <- top_vars$variavel

# 5. Plotar PCA só com essas variáveis
sel_traits = fviz_pca_var(
  pca_res,
  col.var = "contrib",
  gradient.cols = c("#2E5BFF","purple","#FF1E00"),
  repel = T,
  labelsize = 20,
  arrowsize =4 ,
  col.circle = "grey77",
  select.var = list(name = top_names),  # 👈 só as escolhidas
  title = NULL) + theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +  
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),   # 👈 eixo Y em negrito
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24)) +
  labs(title = NULL)
sel_traits
```

```{r}
biplotlbi = biplotlbi + ggtitle("A")
sel_traits = sel_traits + ggtitle("B")
joinplots2 = biplotlbi / sel_traits
joinplots2
```

```{r}
ggsave("joinplots2.tiff", joinplots2, width = 8, height = 14, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw",            # compressão sem perdas
  units = "in", bg = "transparent")
```

```{r}
correlation = read.xlsx("C://Users//joaop//Desktop//fava//corr.xlsx")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Seus dados organizados
cor_df <- tribble(
  ~Trait,              ~Method,     ~Correlation,
  "GLI_E04",           "Spearman",   0.6002259,
  "GLI_E06",           "Spearman",   0.5742518,
  "BGI_E03",           "Spearman",  -0.5652174,
  "BGI_E03_rep",       "Spearman",  -0.5234331,
  "BGI_E04",           "Spearman",  -0.5008470,
  "GLI_E05",           "Spearman",   0.5008470,
  "BGI_E05",           "Spearman",  -0.4760023,
  "PC1",               "Spearman",   0.5268210,
  "AUC_BGI_12.5m",     "Pearson",   -0.4889659,
  "AUC_%Canopy_12.5m", "Pearson",    0.5318657,
  "AUC_BGI_25m",       "Pearson",   -0.4712147,
  "AUC_GLI_25m",       "Pearson",    0.5037733
)
```

```{r}
correlation = ggplot(cor_df, aes(x = reorder(Trait, Correlation), y = Correlation, fill = Method)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = TRUE, width = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Spearman" = "#2E5BFF", "Pearson" = "orange")) +
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +
  labs(x = "Trait", y = "Correlation with HSW (g)",
       title = NULL) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),   # 👈 eixo Y em negrito
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24))
correlation
```

```{r}
correlation = correlation + ggtitle("C")
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "correlation.tiff",
  plot = correlation,
  width = 12,
  height = 14,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300,
  compression = "lzw", bg = "transparent"
)
```

```{r}
joinplots4 = (biplotlbi / sel_traits) | correlation
joinplots4
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(glmnet)
library(xgboost)
library(randomForest)
# Dados filtrados da PC1 e PC2 (8 traits)
dados <- blups_traits_sig2
resposta <- "HSW"
vars_sig_weight <- c("BGI_E06", "BGI_E05", "BGI_E03", "GLI_E05", "GLI_E06","AUC_BGI_12.5m", "AUC_BGI_25m", "AUC_GLI_25m")
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "LOOCV", savePredictions = "final")
dados_model <- dados %>% select(all_of(resposta), all_of(vars_sig_weight)) %>% drop_na()
f <- as.formula(paste(resposta, "~ ."))
set.seed(123)
rf <- train(f, data = dados_model, method = "rf", trControl = ctrl, metric = "RMSE")
set.seed(123)
xgb <- train(f, data = dados_model, method = "xgbTree", trControl = ctrl, preProcess = c("center", "scale"))
set.seed(123)
lm_mod <- train(f, data = dados_model, method = "lm", trControl = ctrl)
final_results <- tibble(
  Trait = resposta,
  RF_R2 = R2(rf$pred$pred, rf$pred$obs),
  RF_RMSE = RMSE(rf$pred$pred, rf$pred$obs),
  XGB_R2 = R2(xgb$pred$pred, xgb$pred$obs),
  XGB_RMSE = RMSE(xgb$pred$pred, xgb$pred$obs),
  LM_R2 = R2(lm_mod$pred$pred, lm_mod$pred$obs),
  LM_RMSE = RMSE(lm_mod$pred$pred, lm_mod$pred$obs)
)
print(final_results)
```

```{r}
# Criar tabela de anotações com R² e RMSE
annot_df <- tibble(
  Model = c("RF", "XGB", "LM"),
  R2 = c(final_results$RF_R2, final_results$XGB_R2, final_results$LM_R2),
  RMSE = c(final_results$RF_RMSE, final_results$XGB_RMSE, final_results$LM_RMSE),
  x_pos = min(pred_df$obs),   # posição x do texto
  y_pos = max(pred_df$pred)   # posição y do texto
) 
pal <- c("RF" = "#2E5BFF", "XGB" = "#3DF200", "LM" = "orange")
# Adicionar no gráfico
performance = ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = obs, y = pred, color = Model)) +
  geom_point(size = 10, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~Model, ncol = 3) +
  geom_text(data = annot_df, 
            aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, 
                label = paste0("R² = ", round(R2,2), "\nRMSE = ", round(RMSE,2))),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, vjust = 1.2, size = 12, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +
  labs(
    x = "Observed HSW",
    y = "Predicted HSW",
    title = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 24),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 32),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
performance 
```

```{r}
ggsave("performance.tiff", performance, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw", units = "in", bg = "transparent")
```

```{r}
ggsave("performance2.png", performance, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "transparent")
```

```{r}
       width = 30, height = 40, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
       width = 28, height = 40, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
ggsave("poster_plots3.tiff", final_plot,
       width = 35, height = 45, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)   # 👈 para manipular legendas

# Reorganizar dados em formato longo
lbispats_long <- lbispats %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(R, C, `f(Column)`, `f(Row)`, 
                        `f(Column):Row`, `Column:f(Row)`, `f(Column):f(Row)`),
               names_to = "Component",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    DAS = as.character(DAS),
    Env = as.character(Env)
  )

# ordem do eixo X
ordem_DAS <- c("23","40","64","118-133")

# ordem dos componentes
ordem_comp <- c("R","C","f(Column)","f(Row)","f(Column):Row","Column:f(Row)","f(Column):f(Row)")

# paleta de cores contrastantes
cores = c("R" = "#6BBFFF", "C" = "#FFAC6B","f(Column)" = "#3DF200","f(Row)" = "#AB6BFF", "f(Column):Row" = "#FF6B75", "Column:f(Row)" = "#F2B600", "f(Column):f(Row)" = "#A35A00")

# Função para gerar um gráfico de um Trait específico
make_plot <- function(tr){
  df_trait <- lbispats_long %>% filter(Trait == tr)
  
  df_trait <- df_trait %>% 
    mutate(DAS = factor(DAS, levels = intersect(ordem_DAS, unique(DAS))))
  
  pef <- ggplot(df_trait, aes(x = DAS, y = Value,
                            color = Component,
                            group = interaction(Component, Env))) +
    geom_line(size = 1, alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 3, stroke = 1.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15, "25m" = 16, "*" = 8))+
                       #guide = "none") +  # 👈 esconde legenda de Env
    scale_color_manual(values = cores, breaks = ordem_comp) +
    theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),                  
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right",   # 👈 sem legenda individual
      plot.title = element_text(face="bold"),
      aspect.ratio = 1
    ) +
    labs(x = "DAS", y = "EDk") +
    facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y")   # 👈 mantém fundo preto nos títulos
  return(pef)
}

# Ordem desejada dos traits (3x3)
ordem_traits <- list(
  c("AUC_%Canopy", "AUC_BGI", "AUC_GLI"),
  c("%Canopy", "BGI", "GLI"),
  c("HSW (g)", "NGRDI", "VARI")
)

# Criar os 9 gráficos
plotspef <- lapply(unlist(ordem_traits), make_plot)
```

```{r}
ninetraits <- wrap_plots(plotspef_no_legend, ncol = 3, nrow = 3) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect")  # 👈 coleta legendas

# 3️⃣ Adicionar legenda global manualmente
legend_global <- get_legend(
  plotspef[[1]] + plotspef[[2]] + plotspef[[7]] + 
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
      legend.text  = element_text(size = 18),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")
    )
)

gfcs = plot_grid(
  ninetraits, legend_global,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.08)  # ajustar altura da legenda
)
```

```{r}
library(cowplot)
library(patchwork)

# 1️⃣ Remover legendas individuais
plotspef_no_legend <- lapply(plotspef, function(p) p + theme(legend.position = "none"))

# 2️⃣ Criar um plot “dummy” apenas para coletar a legenda global
legend_global <- get_legend(
  plotspef[[5]] +
    geom_point(aes(shape = Env), size = 3, stroke = 1.5) +  # adiciona shapes do Env
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("12.5m" = 15, "25m" = 16, "*" = 8)) +
    guides(
      color = guide_legend(title = "Component"),
      shape = guide_legend(title = "Env")
    ) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
      legend.text  = element_text(size = 18),
      legend.key.size = unit(0, "cm"),
      legend.spacing.x = unit(0.0, "cm")
    )
)

# 3️⃣ Montar os plots com a legenda global compacta
ninetraits <- wrap_plots(plotspef_no_legend, ncol = 3, nrow = 3)

final_plot <- plot_grid(
  ninetraits,
  legend_global,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.05)  # legenda bem compacta
)
```

```{r}
eds = eds + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())
p2 = eds  h2_cvgcve
```

```{r}
library(patchwork)
eds = eds + theme(axis.title.x  = element_blank())
# Linha 1: ninetraits e p2
row1 <- eds / h2_cvgcve
row1 <- row1 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1)) 
# Linha 2: biplotlbi e sel_traits
row2 <- biplotlbi / sel_traits
row2 <- row2 + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))

# Linha 3: correlation e performance
row3 <- correlation | performance
row3 <- row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1))

# Combinar todas as linhas verticalmente
fp3 <- (row1 | row2) / row3
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1),
                         widths = c(1, 1))  # ajuste proporcional vertical

```

```{r}
ggsave("poster_fp5522.tiff", fp3, width = 34, height = 36, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Linha 1: ninetraits e p2
final_plot_A <- final_plot + 
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "A", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

eds_B <- eds + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "B", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

h2_cvgcve_C <- h2_cvgcve +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "C", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

p2 <- eds_B / h2_cvgcve_C

row1 <- final_plot_A | p2
row1 <- row1 + plot_layout(widths = c(1.5, 1))

# Linha 2: biplotlbi e sel_traits
biplotlbi_D <- biplotlbi + 
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "D", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

sel_traits_E <- sel_traits +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "E", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

row2 <- biplotlbi_D | sel_traits_E
row2 <- row2 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1))

# Linha 3: correlation e performance
correlation_F <- correlation +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "F", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

performance_G <- performance +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "G", hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.2,
           size = 8, fontface = "bold")

row3 <- correlation_F | performance_G
row3 <- row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1))

# Combinar todas as linhas verticalmente
fp3_manual <- row1 / row2 / row3
fp3_manual <- fp3_manual + plot_layout(heights = c(2, 2, 1))  # ajuste proporcional vertical

```

```{r}
ggsave("row1.tiff", row1, width = 10, height = 14, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```



```{r}
library(patchwork)
# Linha 1: ninetraits e p2
row1 <- eds / h2_cvgcve
# Linha 2: biplotlbi e sel_traits
row2 <- biplotlbi / sel_traits
row2 <- row2 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))

# Linha 3: correlation e performance
row3 <- correlation  | performance
row3 <- row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))
# Combinar todas as linhas verticalmente
fp3 <- (row1 | row2) / row3
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_layout(heights = c(3,1), widths = c(3,1))  # ajuste proporcional vertical
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Exemplo de ajuste de tema para remover espaço extra à esquerda
theme_no_margin <- theme(
  axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 2)),  # r = margem direita do título y
  plot.margin  = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0, unit = "pt")  # remove margem total
)

# Aplicar aos gráficos à esquerda
eds <- eds + theme_no_margin
h2_cvgcve <- h2_cvgcve + theme_no_margin
biplotlbi <- biplotlbi + theme_no_margin
sel_traits <- sel_traits + theme_no_margin

# Combinar linhas
row1 <- eds / h2_cvgcve
row2 <- biplotlbi / sel_traits
row2 <- row2 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))
row3 <- correlation | performance
row3 <- row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))

fp3 <- (row1 | row2) / row3
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_layout(heights = c(3,1), widths = c(3,1))

fp3

```

```{r}
ggsave("bvbvmmxkkmmmxaaasab.tiff",fp3, width = 36 , height = 36, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
rf_df <- rf$pred %>% mutate(Model = "RF")
xgb_df <- xgb$pred %>% mutate(Model = "XGB")
lm_df <- lm_mod$pred %>% mutate(Model = "LM")

pred_df <- bind_rows(rf_df, xgb_df, lm_df)
annot_df <- tibble(
  Model = c("RF", "XGB", "LM"),
  R2 = c(final_results$RF_R2, final_results$XGB_R2, final_results$LM_R2),
  RMSE = c(final_results$RF_RMSE, final_results$XGB_RMSE, final_results$LM_RMSE),
  x_pos = min(pred_df$obs),
  y_pos = max(pred_df$pred)
)
performance = ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = obs, y = pred, color = Model)) +
  geom_point(size = 6, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~Model, ncol = 3) +
  geom_text(data = annot_df, 
            aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, 
                label = paste0("R² = ", round(R2,2), "\nRMSE = ", round(RMSE,2))),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, vjust = 1.2, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +
  labs(
    x = "Observed HSW",
    y = "Predicted HSW",
    title = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 24),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
performance
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)

# ---- Tema para remover margens extras ----
theme_no_margin <- theme(
  axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = ggplot2::margin(r = 2)), 
  axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
  axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
  panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0, unit = "pt")
)

# ---- Aplicar tema aos gráficos individuais ----
eds <- eds + theme_no_margin
h2_cvgcve <- h2_cvgcve + theme_no_margin
biplotlbi <- biplotlbi + theme_no_margin
sel_traits <- sel_traits + theme_no_margin
correlation <- correlation + theme_no_margin
performance <- performance + theme_no_margin

# ---- Combinar linhas ----
row1 <- eds / h2_cvgcve
row1 <- row1 + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))  

row2 <- biplotlbi / sel_traits
row2 <- row2 + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))

row3 <- correlation | performance
row3 <- row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1))

# ---- Combinar todas as linhas verticalmente ----
fp3 <- (row1 | row2) / row3
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_layout(heights = c(3, 1, 1), widths = c(3, 1, 1))

# ---- Adicionar letras (A, B, C...) em negrito ----
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_annotation(
  tag_levels = "A",   # gera A, B, C...
  tag_suffix = "",    # remove parênteses
  theme = theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28)  # aumenta tamanho para pôster
  )
)

# ---- Salvar em PDF vetorial (ideal para pôster) ----
ggsave("fp3_plot.pdf", fp3, width = 16, height = 12)  # ajuste dimensões conforme seu pôster

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)

# ---- Tema para remover margens extras e deixar tudo em negrito ----
theme_no_margin <- theme(
  axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = ggplot2::margin(r = 2)), 
  axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
  axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
  panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0, unit = "pt")
)

# ---- Aplicar tema aos gráficos individuais ----
eds <- eds + theme_no_margin
h2_cvgcve <- h2_cvgcve + theme_no_margin
biplotlbi <- biplotlbi + theme_no_margin
sel_traits <- sel_traits + theme_no_margin
correlation <- correlation + theme_no_margin
performance <- performance + theme_no_margin

# ---- Combinar gráficos em layout vertical ----
row1 <- eds | h2_cvgcve
row2 <- biplotlbi | sel_traits
row3 <- correlation | performance
row3 = row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1,2))
fp3 <- (row1 / row2 )/ row3
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_layout(heights = c(3,2,1))

# ---- Letras grandes para cada gráfico ----
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_annotation(
  tag_levels = "A",   # gera A, B, C...
  tag_suffix = "",
  theme = theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 36)  # maior para pôster vertical
  )
)

# ---- Salvar para pôster vertical ----
ggsave("fp3_vertical.tiff", fp3, width = 30, height = 30, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")  # largura x altura do 
```

```{r}
ggsave("row1.tiff", row1, width = 32, height = 18, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")  # largura x altura do 
```

```{r}
ggsave("row2.tiff", row2, width = 32, height = 18, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")  # largura x altura do 
```

```{r}
ggsave("row3.tiff", row3, width = 32, height = 12, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")  # largura x altura do 
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# ---- Tema para remover margens extras e deixar tudo em negrito ----
theme_no_margin <- theme(
  axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", margin = ggplot2::margin(r = 2)), 
  axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
  axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black"),
  panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0, unit = "pt")
)

# ---- Aplicar tema aos gráficos individuais ----
plots <- list(eds, h2_cvgcve, biplotlbi, sel_traits, correlation, performance)
plots <- lapply(plots, function(p) p + theme_no_margin)
eds <- plots[[1]]; h2_cvgcve <- plots[[2]]
biplotlbi <- plots[[3]]; sel_traits <- plots[[4]]
correlation <- plots[[5]]; performance <- plots[[6]]

# ---- Montar linhas horizontais ----
row1 <- eds | h2_cvgcve
row1 <- row1 + plot_layout(widths = c(1,1))  # ajustar proporção horizontal

row2 <- biplotlbi | sel_traits
row2 <- row2 + plot_layout(widths = c(1,2))  # largura diferente

row3 <- correlation | performance
row3 <- row3 + plot_layout(widths = c(1,2))

# ---- Empilhar as linhas verticalmente ----
fp3 <- row1 / row2 / row3
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_layout(heights = c(3,2,2))  # proporção vertical

# ---- Adicionar letras grandes ----
fp3 <- fp3 + plot_annotation(
  tag_levels = "A",
  tag_suffix = "",
  theme = theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 36))
)

# ---- Salvar ----
ggsave("fp3_vertical.tiff", fp3, width = 30, height = 40, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
library(cowplot)

# Combinar 2x3 plots
top_row <- plot_grid(eds, h2_cvgcve, ncol = 2, labels = c("A", "B"), label_size = 30)
middle_row <- plot_grid(biplotlbi, sel_traits, ncol = 2, labels = c("C", "D"), label_size = 30)
bottom_row <- plot_grid(correlation, performance, ncol = 2, labels = c("E", "F"), label_size = 30)

# Combinar tudo verticalmente
final_plot <- plot_grid(top_row, middle_row, bottom_row, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(3, 2, 2))

# Salvar
ggsave("final_cowplot.tiff", final_plot, width = 32, height = 44, dpi = 600)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

theme_poster <- theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) + 
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt")
  )

eds <- eds + theme_poster
h2_cvgcve <- h2_cvgcve + theme_poster
biplotlbi <- biplotlbi + theme_poster
sel_traits <- sel_traits + theme_poster
correlation <- correlation + theme_poster
performance <- performance + theme_poster
top_row <- plot_grid(eds, h2_cvgcve, ncol = 2, labels = c("A", "B"), label_size = 36)
middle_row <- plot_grid(biplotlbi, sel_traits, ncol = 2, labels = c("C", "D"), label_size = 36)
bottom_row <- plot_grid(correlation, performance, ncol = 2, labels = c("E", "F"), label_size = 36)
final_plot <- plot_grid(
  top_row, middle_row, bottom_row,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(3, 2, 2)  # ajustar proporção de cada linha
)
ggsave("final_plot_poster.tiff", final_plot, width = 32, height = 45, dpi = 600)

```

```{r}
# Tema para pôster
theme_poster <- theme_linedraw(base_size = 28) +   # aumenta todo o texto
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 32),
    axis.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28, color = "black"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 34, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 26, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

# Aplicar em todos os gráficos ANTES de juntar
eds        <- eds + theme_poster
h2_cvgcve  <- h2_cvgcve + theme_poster
biplotlbi  <- biplotlbi + theme_poster
sel_traits <- sel_traits + theme_poster
correlation <- correlation + theme_poster
performance <- performance + theme_poster

```

```{r}
row1 <- eds / h2_cvgcve
row2 <- biplotlbi / sel_traits + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))
row3 <- correlation | performance + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))

fp3 <- (row1 |row2) / row3 +
  plot_layout(heights = c(3, 2))  # proporção vertical

```

```{r}
ggsave("fp3_poster.tiff", fp3, width = 36, height = 40, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")

```

```{r}
ggsave("fp1.tiff", eds, width = 16, height = 20, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
ggsave("f2.tiff", h2_cvgcve, width = 16, height = 20, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
ggsave("f3.tiff", biplotlbi, width = 16, height = 16, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
ggsave("f4.tiff",sel_traits, width = 16, height = 16, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
ggsave("f5.tiff",correlation, width = 14, height = 16, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
ggsave("f6.tiff",performance, width = 16, height = 16, dpi = 600, compression = "lzw")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# 1. Tema único para pôster
tema_poster <- theme_linedraw(base_size = 32) + 
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 30, angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 30),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30),
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30),
    strip.text   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30)
  )

# 2. Aplicar tema em todos os gráficos
eds        <- eds        + tema_poster
h2_cvgcve  <- h2_cvgcve  + tema_poster
biplotlbi  <- biplotlbi  + tema_poster
sel_traits <- sel_traits + tema_poster
correlation<- correlation+ tema_poster
performance<- performance+ tema_poster

# 3. Organizar em 3 linhas x 2 colunas
final_plot <- (eds | h2_cvgcve) /
              (sel_traits  | correlation) /
              (performance) + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 0.8))

# 4. Exportar — metade da altura do pôster (~45 cm de altura)
ggsave("post04esad22ada.tiff", 
       final_plot, 
       width = 100, height = 120, units = "cm", dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
pca_contrib = fviz_pca_var(
  pca_res,
  col.var = "contrib", # Colorir pelas contribuições
  gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB","purple","darkred"),
  repel = TRUE,
  labelsize = 14,
  arrowsize = 2,
  col.circle = "grey77",
  select.var = list(contrib = 8), 
  title = NULL) + theme_linedraw(base_size = 24) +  
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),   # 👈 eixo Y em negrito
    legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24)) +
  labs(title = NULL)
pca_contrib
```
